classes.reasoning: The document explicitly identifies itself as 'PARTICIPANT INFORMATION AND INFORMED CONSENT FORM' with signature blocks for participant and investigator consent, consent checkboxes with initials fields, and the email header confirms 'APPROVED ICF v1.0' distribution to site coordinators.

trial_title: A Phase III Study of BRC-883 (Cardanezumab) in Patients with Familial High Cholesterol Who Cannot Take Statins
summary: This is the Ethics Committee-approved master Informed Consent Form version 1.0 (dated 01 March 2024) for study BIORCE-CAR-2024-007, distributed to site coordinators for use with newly enrolled patients. The ICF explains the Phase III study of BRC-883 (Cardanezumab) in patients with familial high cholesterol who cannot tolerate statins, describes study procedures (52 weeks treatment plus 24 weeks follow-up), possible risks, and obtains participant consent via signature and initialed checkboxes.
